symptoms.symptom_name: Cluster of prolonged tonic seizures without regaining consciousness between them

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Recurrent seizures without inter-ictal recovery is the operational definition of impending/early status epilepticus.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden loss of all muscle tone (atonic drop attack) causing a fall

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Atonic drop attacks are a hallmark seizure type of Lennox-Gastaut syndrome and a major cause of injury.

symptoms.symptom_name: Post-ictal obtundation — drowsy, floppy, not visually tracking

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Failure to return to baseline alertness between seizures indicates ongoing cerebral compromise and airway risk.
Reduced consciousness after head trauma is non-specific but keeps intracranial injury in play.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep bleeding laceration over the left supraorbital ridge after the fall

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Directly observed wound; also the visible marker of head impact prompting intracranial injury assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile — no fever or preceding illness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Febrile seizures require fever; its absence removes this benign explanation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known Lennox-Gastaut syndrome on valproate, lamotrigine and clobazam

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Refractory epileptic encephalopathies carry a high baseline risk of prolonged seizures and status epilepticus.
A missed benzodiazepine-class antiepileptic is a recognised precipitant of breakthrough seizure clusters.

symptoms.symptom_name: No rescue benzodiazepine given yet during the cluster

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Untreated cluster time is the key modifiable driver of progression to established status.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia HR 130, BP 110/70, RR 24, SpO2 96%, temperature 37.5 (clinician-measured)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sympathetic surge (tachycardia) accompanies prolonged seizure activity; sealed clinician measurement — the AI infers distress from history, not from a measured vital.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pupils equal and reactive to light

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Equal reactive pupils make a large mass-effect haemorrhage less likely but do not exclude intracranial injury — a clinician/CT finding, not telehealth-obtainable.

symptoms.symptom_name: Interictal EEG slow spike-and-wave complexes (<2.5 Hz)

investigation_details.expected_result: Slow (<2.5 Hz) generalised spike-and-wave, characteristic of Lennox-Gastaut syndrome
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Not an acute test; relevant only to baseline diagnosis, never to the emergency and never available in real-time telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Slow spike-and-wave is a defining EEG feature of LGS — a hospital/outpatient investigation, not telehealth-obtainable.

symptoms.symptom_name: CT head to exclude intracranial haemorrhage

investigation_details.expected_result: To exclude traumatic intracranial haemorrhage after the fall
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: An intracranial bleed could be missed; a hospital investigation, not obtainable via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Imaging is the only reliable way to exclude intracranial haemorrhage after head trauma with reduced consciousness.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Impending status epilepticus in refractory epilepsy
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A cluster of prolonged seizures without recovery, in a child with refractory epilepsy and no rescue medication given, is a time-critical emergency mandating abortive therapy and an ambulance.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Exclude traumatic intracranial haemorrhage after a witnessed head impact with reduced consciousness — a hospital task, requested via escalation, not performed in telehealth.
Hypoglycaemia is a rapidly reversible cause/aggravator of seizures and must be checked in any prolonged paediatric seizure — a paramedic/point-of-care task.
Assess adherence and subtherapeutic levels contributing to breakthrough seizures — a hospital blood test.
Relevant to baseline epilepsy characterisation only; not an acute investigation and must not delay escalation.
No features of CNS infection and the child is afebrile; routine LP is not indicated and would be an over-investigation in this acute trauma-plus-seizure context.
